intro: Agathis moorei, also known as Daintree Rosewood, is a large evergreen tree native to Australia, belonging to the family Araucariaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing pain, fever, and infections. Bioactive constituents of Agathis moorei include terpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Agathis moorei is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
